\newcommand{\highlight}[1]{\textbf{#1}}
\newcommand{\todo}[1]{\textcolor{red}{TODO: #1}}
\newcommand{\R}{\mathbb{R}}

\title{Sample LaTeX Document}
\author{Test Author}

\begin{document}

\maketitle
\tableofcontents

\section{Introduction}
This document demonstrates LaTeX parsing for code intelligence.
We use \highlight{custom commands} throughout.

\subsection{Background}
Previous work on code search has focused on keyword matching.
Semantic search using embeddings provides better recall.

\subsection{Motivation}
Understanding document structure helps with navigation and search.

\section{Methods}

\subsection{Parsing Strategy}
We use tree-sitter to parse LaTeX documents into ASTs.
Each section becomes a searchable chunk.

\subsection{Embedding Generation}
Section content is embedded using transformer models.

\begin{theorem}
For any function $f: \R \to \R$ that is continuous on $[a,b]$,
there exists $c \in (a,b)$ such that $f'(c) = \frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a}$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Apply Rolle's theorem to the auxiliary function
$g(x) = f(x) - f(a) - \frac{f(b)-f(a)}{b-a}(x-a)$.
\end{proof}

\section{Results}
Our approach achieves 90.9\% Recall@1 on code search benchmarks.

\section{Conclusion}
Semantic code search with local embeddings is effective and practical.

\end{thebibliography}
